entry.title: Architecture Gone Wrong: Leveraging SAP Architecture Center for Better Outcomes
entry.content:
In the today growing software development, architecture plays a crucial role in determining the success and efficiency of a system. Poor architecture can lead to significant costs, performance issues, and maintenance headaches.

This blog explores the pitfalls of bad architecture and highlights the advantages of leveraging the SAP Architecture Center to ensure best practices and cost savings.

The Pitfalls of Poor Architecture

1. High Costs: Poorly designed architecture can lead to increased costs in several ways:

  • Maintenance: Systems with bad architecture require frequent fixes and updates, leading to higher maintenance costs.
  • Scalability: Inefficient architecture can struggle to scale, necessitating expensive overhauls or replacements.
  • Performance: Suboptimal architecture can result in slow performance, requiring additional resources to meet performance standards.

2. Performance Issues: Bad architecture can severely impact system performance:

  • Latency: Inefficient data flow and processing can lead to high latency, affecting user experience.
  • Reliability: Poor architecture can result in frequent downtimes and failures, reducing system reliability.
  • Resource Utilization: Inefficient use of resources can lead to higher operational costs and lower performance.

The SAP Architecture Center: A Solution

The SAP Architecture Center offers a comprehensive suite of tools, best practices, and reference architectures to help businesses design efficient and cost-effective systems

https://architecture.learning.sap.com 

Here are some key benefits:

1. Best Practices: The SAP Architecture Center provides access to industry best practices, ensuring that your architecture is designed to meet the highest standards. This includes guidelines for security, performance, and scalability.

2. Reference Architectures: Reference architectures are templates that outline the interactions between various services and components. They provide a standardized approach to designing systems, ensuring optimal configurations and seamless integration 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchCatalog/ 

3. Community-Driven Guidance: The SAP Architecture Center fosters a community of practice where architects and developers can share insights, collaborate, and learn from each other. This collaborative environment helps in adopting proven frameworks and innovative solutions

https://architecture.learning.sap.com/community/intro 

Advantages and Benefits

1. Cost Savings: By following SAP best practices and utilizing reference architectures, businesses can significantly reduce costs:

  • Reduced Maintenance: Well-designed architecture requires less frequent fixes and updates.
  • Efficient Resource Utilization: Optimal configurations ensure efficient use of resources, reducing operational costs.
  • Scalability: Scalable architecture minimizes the need for expensive overhauls.

2. Improved Performance: Leveraging the SAP Architecture Center can lead to better system performance:

  • Lower Latency: Efficient data flow and processing reduce latency.
  • Higher Reliability: Robust architecture ensures fewer downtimes and failures.
  • Optimal Resource Utilization: Efficient use of resources leads to better performance.

FinOps for Cost Saving

Financial Operations (FinOps) is a practice that combines financial management with cloud operations to optimize costs. Integrating FinOps with SAP best practices can further enhance cost savings:

  1. Cost Monitoring: FinOps tools help in monitoring cloud spending, identifying cost drivers, and optimizing resource usage.
  2. Budgeting and Forecasting: FinOps practices enable accurate budgeting and forecasting, ensuring that financial resources are allocated efficiently.
  3. Continuous Improvement: FinOps encourages continuous improvement by regularly reviewing and optimizing cloud spending, leading to sustained cost savings.

Conclusion

Poor architecture can lead to significant costs and performance issues. However, by leveraging the SAP Architecture Center and adopting FinOps practices, businesses can design efficient, cost-effective, and high-performing systems. The SAP Architecture Center provides best practices, reference architectures, and community-driven guidance to ensure that your architecture is built to the highest standards. Embrace these tools and practices to avoid the pitfalls of bad architecture and achieve optimal results.

entry.title: Re: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content:
It looks like you are passing the values of the custom fields as offset data in the VALUEPART1 field of the EXTENSIONIN parameter.

If so, I believe you would need to copy those values from this structure into matching fields of EKPO using a badi implementation.

Take a look at the method EXTENSIONIN of the badi ME_BAPI_PO_IBS. I think you need to fill your custom fields in POITEM as the data from function module BAPI_PO_CHANGE is processed.

Did you add your custom fields to structure MEPOITEM as well as to EKPO?

entry.title: Re: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content: Method EXTENSIONIN of Badi ME_BAPI_PO_CUST might be a better choice.

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA - How to develop an SAPUI5 app calling a back...
entry.content:
By definition, a SAPUI5 app is a web app so it runs in the browser, regardless if’s consuming data from S/4HANA, ECC, CRM or any other SAP or non SAP system. 
as the app runs in user’s browser, you cannot select data like in an classic ABAP application (I guess this is what you mean by “ECC” in your question).

so, you need a REST API - regardless it’s OData or not. By if your backend is an S/4HANA system creating an API using ABAP (Using RAP for recent versions specially) is the easiest and default choice

entry.title: LMS Data Story Report
entry.content:
I am trying to create a story report based on LMS data that shows the total amount of time spent in courses by our employees. I am summing on the duration field. However, many employees have been assigned a course multiple times, despite completing it only once so the duration is being overstated when added. 

For example, I have records like:

Employee#Course NameAssignment DateCompletion DateDuration
1234Course ABCJan. 1, 2025May 31, 202510
1234 Course ABCFeb. 15, 2025May 31, 202510
1234Course ABCMar. 15, 2025May 31, 202510

In the above example, the employee completed the course once and so I want to show the duration as 10, not 30. Does anyone know of a way of accomplishing this? Is there a way to filter the query or the table on the max of completion date for the same employee id and course name?

entry.title: Suggestions to execute mass stock relocation without manually creating a WT for each item
entry.content:
We are relocating stock within the same warehouse. The relocation involves new storage types and bins but within the same warehouse number.

We want to:

Physically move all materials from old bins to new ones

Record article numbers, quantities, base unit of measure, and new vs old storage bins

Avoid creating individual warehouse tasks manually for each article

We are seeking guidance on the following:

Which SAP Fiori application should we use in S/4HANA Cloud to perform this kind of mass stock transfer?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Suggestions to execute mass stock relocation without...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In addition to the Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/mass-stock-relocation-in-warehouse-without-creating-individual-warehouse/qaq-p/14132251

I would recommend checking the ‘Transfer Stock – In-Plant’ app provides functionality for transferring stock between storage locations or bins within the same plant. It offers a guided process where the user can select materials, specify source and target storage locations, and confirm quantities to be transferred. 

Also, I would recommend checking the ‘Post Goods Movement’ app, that allows users to perform a wide range of goods movements, including goods receipts, issues, and transfer postings. It offers flexibility for different movement types and is often used in processes that involve adjustments, consumption postings, or more complex movement scenarios. 

However, neither app provides standard functionality to import an Excel file and automatically process multiple stock transfers. For bulk processing, additional development or automation would be necessary.

In addition, 

For more information:

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant (SAP Fiori Apps Library)

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant (SAP Help Portal)

  • Post Goods Movement (SAP Fiori Apps Library)

  • Post Goods Movement (SAP Help Portal)

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Variable pay based on different base dates in prorated template
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a requirement where in bonus period is 1/Aug/2024 – 31/July/2025. If the user doesn’t have any change in salary after 1st October, then the 1st October salary should be used for the entire year but if the user has any salary change after 1st October(offcycle increase) then the system should calculate as per the dates. Below is the sample data.

RQ2 and RQ3 will work as expected with standard proration is my understanding but to achieve RQ1 do we have any standard solution from SAP

We thought of a solution by creating a MDF object and populate all the salaries from 1st Aug till July 31st and update all the 1st Aug till 31st  Sep salary to October 1st salary value in MDF object for users who don’t have a salary change after 1st October and use this value in the VP history, Since this involves a lot a manual process, could you please let us know if we have a standard process to achieve this behavior in a EC Variable pay template.

Kindly provide details if anyone has experience implementing such a requirement

 

Req No

Business Requirement

Date

Date

Date

Date

Date

Expectation

 

 

1-Aug

1-Sep

1-Oct

1-May

31-Jul

 

RQ1

Standard

100,000 

100,000 

105,000 

105,000 

105,000

We use $105,000 for the full 365 period

RQ2

Out of Cycle 1

100,000 

100,000 

105,000

110,000

110,000

We use $100,000 for August and September, $105,000 for October to April, $110,000 for May to July assignment

RQ3

Out of Cycle 2

100,000 

110,000 

110,000 

110,000 

110,000

We use $100,000 for August, $110,000 for the second rest of year assignment

Regards,

Siva

entry.title: Re: Modify SFTP Report
entry.content: Just so I understand your response correctly, I would need to stop the extraction of the FTP report configuration that is configured in the "Report_System" configuration. Then, I would need to create a new recurring report by navigating to the "Reports" section of the LMS and applying the necessary filters to the "UserItemStatusCSV" report. After doing this, would I then be able to configure the FTP report configuration through the "Report_System" configuration to specifically extract my newly created report with the added filters? Or are you saying that the "Report_System" report FTP configuration cannot do that at all, and the only way to extract the specific data I need is to go through the "Reports" section of the LMS to modify and schedule the "UserItemStatusCSV" report? I hope this makes sense.

entry.title: Re: This document could not be opened error
entry.content: you need to right click on the exe and select Run as Adminstrator. there is no tool to "fix" reports, you should always backup your reports before attempting any more updates.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio / Delete Workspace
entry.content:
Hi Matthias,

To delete the unused workspaces:

1. First open the BAS and click on the 3 lines symbol then File > Open Folder  

2.Give your path to projects folder. Generally for everyone the path would be the same :/home/user/projects

And open till projects path only!

3. Then Select the projects folder. 

4.Then you can find your projects & workspaces 

5.Select the workspace which you want to delete and right click on it. You can find the option "Delete Permanently"


Note : Make sure you are doing this in the explorer but not in any workspace 


I hope this solves your issue.

Thanks,

Karthik

entry.title: UPLOAD DATA FROM EXCEL TO ANY TABLE Z IN SAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I'm locking for FM or anyway for check validate field about DATS, TIMS, CURR, CURK,... and Value Range in internal table because I want to upload data from excel to any Z table data. So I need help to validate data before upload into internal table Z*. Here is my code correct and can upload any table Z but it doesn't validate data to internal table.

FORM UPLOAD_DATA_FROM_EXCEL.
  DATA: LT_FILE_TABLE    TYPE FILETABLE,
        LV_RC            TYPE I,
        LV_FILE_PATH     TYPE STRING,
        LV_FILE_LENGTH   TYPE I,
        LV_XSTRING       TYPE XSTRING,
        LT_RAW_DATA      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF X255,
        LT_EXCEL_DATA    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LS_EXCEL_LINE    TYPE ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LT_KEY_FIELDS    TYPE TABLE OF DD03L,
        LV_ROW           TYPE I VALUE 1,
        LV_ERROR_COUNT   TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_SUCCESS_COUNT TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_MSG           TYPE STRING.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
           EXCEL_NAME TYPE STRING,
           DDIC_NAME  TYPE FIELDNAME,
           COL_POS    TYPE I, " Excel column position
           DATATYPE   TYPE DATATYPE_D, " Add datatype to mapping
         END OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <LT_ALV>      TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                 <LS_ALV>      TYPE ANY,
                 <LS_EXISTING> TYPE ANY,
                 <LV_FIELD>    TYPE ANY,
                 <LV_IS_NEW>   TYPE ANY,
                 <LT_CELLTAB>  TYPE LVC_T_STYL,
                 <LS_CELLTAB>  TYPE LVC_S_STYL,
                 <LV_LOCKED>   TYPE ANY.

  DATA: LT_FILE_CONTENT TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TEXT255,
        LV_ERROR_TEXT   TYPE STRING,
        LV_MSG_TYPE     TYPE SY-MSGTY,
        LV_MSG_ID       TYPE SY-MSGID,
        LV_MSG_NO       TYPE SY-MSGNO,
        LV_MSG_V1       TYPE SY-MSGV1.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 1. Upload Excel file content
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  CALL METHOD CL_GUI_FRONTEND_SERVICES=>FILE_OPEN_DIALOG
    EXPORTING
      WINDOW_TITLE            = 'Select Excel File'
      FILE_FILTER             = 'Excel Files (*.XLSX)|*.XLSX|'
    CHANGING
      FILE_TABLE              = LT_FILE_TABLE
      RC                      = LV_RC
    EXCEPTIONS
      FILE_OPEN_DIALOG_FAILED = 1
      CNTL_ERROR              = 2
      ERROR_NO_GUI            = 3
      NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_GUI    = 4
      OTHERS                  = 5.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LV_RC = 0 OR LT_FILE_TABLE IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'File selection was cancelled' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'W'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  READ TABLE LT_FILE_TABLE INDEX 1 INTO DATA(LS_FILE).
  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read selected file information' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  LV_FILE_PATH = LS_FILE-FILENAME.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 2. Process Excel content with improved header validation
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  DATA: LV_FILENAME TYPE RLGRAP-FILENAME.
  LV_FILENAME = LV_FILE_PATH.

  " First read only the header row (row 1)
  CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      I_BEGIN_COL             = 1
      I_BEGIN_ROW             = 1
      I_END_COL               = 100
      I_END_ROW               = 1
    TABLES
      INTERN                  = LT_EXCEL_DATA
    EXCEPTIONS
      INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS = 1
      UPLOAD_OLE              = 2
      OTHERS                  = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LT_EXCEL_DATA IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read header from Excel file' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Get the expected fields from data dictionary
  DATA: LT_EXPECTED_FIELDS TYPE TABLE OF DFIES,
        LT_ACTUAL_HEADERS  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF STRING,
        LS_HEADER_LINE     TYPE ALSMEX_TABLINE,
        LV_EXPECTED_FIELD  TYPE DFIES-FIELDNAME,
        LV_HEADER_MISSING  TYPE ABAP_BOOL VALUE ABAP_FALSE,
        LT_FIELD_MAPPING   TYPE TABLE OF TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
        LS_FIELD_MAPPING   TYPE TY_FIELD_MAPPING,
        LT_MISSING_FIELDS  TYPE TABLE OF STRING,
        LT_EXTRA_FIELDS    TYPE TABLE OF STRING.

  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING
      TABNAME        = P_TABLE
    TABLES
      DFIES_TAB      = LT_EXPECTED_FIELDS
    EXCEPTIONS
      NOT_FOUND      = 1
      INTERNAL_ERROR = 2
      OTHERS         = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Could not retrieve expected table fields' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

    "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 2. Process Excel content with STRICT header validation
  "-----------------------------------------------------
  DATA:
    lv_error_msg       TYPE string,
    lv_header_mismatch TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

  " Extract headers from Excel (row 1)
  CLEAR lt_actual_headers.
  LOOP AT lt_excel_data INTO ls_header_line WHERE row = 1.
    IF ls_header_line-value IS INITIAL.
      lv_error_msg = 'Empty header found in column ' && ls_header_line-col && '. All headers must have a value.'.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
    APPEND ls_header_line-value TO lt_actual_headers.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Check for empty headers
  IF lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Validate number of columns
  IF lines( lt_actual_headers ) <> lines( lt_expected_fields ).
    lv_error_msg = |Number of headers ({ lines( lt_actual_headers ) }) does not match target fields ({ lines( lt_expected_fields ) })|.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " STRICT ORDER VALIDATION - Check each header name and position
  LOOP AT lt_expected_fields INTO DATA(ls_expected_field).
    DATA(lv_index) = sy-tabix.

    " Read corresponding Excel header
    READ TABLE lt_actual_headers INTO DATA(lv_header) INDEX lv_index.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      lv_error_msg = |Header at position { lv_index } is missing|.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.

    " Case-insensitive comparison with exact position check
    IF to_upper( lv_header ) <> to_upper( ls_expected_field-fieldname ).
      lv_error_msg = |Header at position { lv_index } is incorrect. Found: "{ lv_header }", Expected: "{ ls_expected_field-fieldname }"|.
      lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
      EXIT.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " If any mismatch, report error and stop
  IF lv_header_mismatch = abap_true.
    MESSAGE lv_error_msg TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

  " Build field mapping between Excel columns and DDIC fields
  CLEAR LT_EXCEL_DATA.
  " Now read the entire Excel file (starting from row 2 for data)
  CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      I_BEGIN_COL             = 1
      I_BEGIN_ROW             = 2 " Skip header row
      I_END_COL               = 100
      I_END_ROW               = 9999
    TABLES
      INTERN                  = LT_EXCEL_DATA
    EXCEPTIONS
      INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS = 1
      UPLOAD_OLE              = 2
      OTHERS                  = 3.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0 OR LT_EXCEL_DATA IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Could not read data from Excel file' TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  "-----------------------------------------------------
  " 3. Get key fields from DD03L and also field types for date/time conversion
  SELECT * FROM DD03L INTO TABLE LT_KEY_FIELDS
    WHERE TABNAME = P_TABLE
      AND KEYFLAG = 'X'
      AND AS4LOCAL = 'A'
      AND AS4VERS = '0000'
    ORDER BY POSITION.

  " Get all fields with their data types for date/time conversion
  DATA: LT_FIELD_TYPES TYPE TABLE OF DFIES.
  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING
      TABNAME        = P_TABLE
    TABLES
      DFIES_TAB      = LT_FIELD_TYPES
    EXCEPTIONS
      NOT_FOUND      = 1
      INTERNAL_ERROR = 2
      OTHERS         = 3.

  " 4. Assign ALV data to field symbol
  ASSIGN GT_ALV_DATA->* TO <LT_ALV>.
  IF <LT_ALV> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
    MESSAGE 'Cannot access ALV data' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " 5. Create structure for key comparison
  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE,
           KEY_STRING TYPE STRING,
           ROW_INDEX  TYPE I,
         END OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE.

  DATA: LT_EXISTING_KEYS TYPE HASHED TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE
                         WITH UNIQUE KEY KEY_STRING,
        LT_NEW_KEYS      TYPE HASHED TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE
                         WITH UNIQUE KEY KEY_STRING,
        LS_KEY           TYPE TY_KEY_STRUCTURE,
        LT_DUPLICATES    TYPE TABLE OF TY_KEY_STRUCTURE.
  " 6. Process Excel data with date/time conversion
  DATA: LV_CURRENT_ROW TYPE I VALUE 0.
  DATA: LV_DATE_STR       TYPE STRING,
        LV_TIME_STR       TYPE STRING,
        LV_CONVERTED_DATE TYPE STRING,
        LV_CONVERTED_TIME TYPE STRING.

  LOOP AT LT_EXCEL_DATA INTO LS_EXCEL_LINE.
    " New row handling
    IF LS_EXCEL_LINE-ROW <> LV_CURRENT_ROW.
      LV_CURRENT_ROW = LS_EXCEL_LINE-ROW.
      LV_ROW = LV_ROW + 1.

      " Add new line to ALV
      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV>.

      " Mark as new record
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_NEW' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_IS_NEW>.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
        <LV_IS_NEW> = ABAP_TRUE.
      ENDIF.

      " Initialize celltab for styling
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CELLTAB' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LT_CELLTAB>.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
        CLEAR <LT_CELLTAB>.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_EXCEL_LINE-COL OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
      <LV_FIELD> = LS_EXCEL_LINE-VALUE.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " 7. Validate keys for new records and handle duplicates by updating existing records
  DATA: LV_START_INDEX     TYPE I,
        LV_END_INDEX       TYPE I,
        LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND TYPE ABAP_BOOL VALUE ABAP_FALSE,
        LV_KEY_MATCH       TYPE ABAP_BOOL,
        LV_UPDATE_COUNT    TYPE I VALUE 0,
        LV_INSERT_COUNT    TYPE I VALUE 0.

  LV_START_INDEX = LINES( <LT_ALV> ) - LV_ROW + 2.
  LV_END_INDEX = LINES( <LT_ALV> ).

  " First pass: check for duplicate primary keys
  LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV> FROM LV_START_INDEX TO LV_END_INDEX.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_NEW' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_IS_NEW>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_IS_NEW> = ABAP_TRUE.
      " Check for duplicate keys in existing data
      LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_EXISTING>.
        " Skip new records being added
        IF SY-TABIX BETWEEN LV_START_INDEX AND LV_END_INDEX.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_DELETED>).
        IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_EXISTING_DELETED> <> 'X'.
          LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_TRUE.

          " Check each key field
          LOOP AT LT_KEY_FIELDS INTO DATA(LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW).
            ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
            ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_KEY_FIELD_NEW-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_FIELD>).
            IF <LV_FIELD> <> <LV_EXISTING_FIELD>.
              LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_FALSE.
              EXIT.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.

          " If keys match, update the existing record with new data
          IF LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_TRUE.
            " Get all non-key fields from the new record
*            LOOP AT LT_FIELD_TYPES INTO DATA(LS_FIELD_TYPE) WHERE KEYFLAG <> 'X'.
*              ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
*              IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_FIELD> IS NOT INITIAL.
*                " Update the existing record with the new value
*                ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_VALUE>).
*                IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
*                  <LV_EXISTING_VALUE> = <LV_FIELD>.
*                ENDIF.
*              ENDIF.
*            ENDLOOP.
            LOOP AT LT_FIELD_TYPES INTO DATA(LS_FIELD_TYPE) WHERE KEYFLAG <> 'X'.
              ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
              IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
                " Always update ALV field (even if Excel field is empty)
                ASSIGN COMPONENT LS_FIELD_TYPE-FIELDNAME OF STRUCTURE <LS_EXISTING> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<LV_EXISTING_VALUE>).
                IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
                  <LV_EXISTING_VALUE> = COND #( WHEN <LV_FIELD> IS INITIAL THEN '' ELSE <LV_FIELD> ).
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDLOOP.

            " Mark the new record as not needed (we've updated the existing one)
            ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
            IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
              <LV_FIELD> = 'X'.
            ENDIF.
            LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND = ABAP_TRUE.
            LV_UPDATE_COUNT = LV_UPDATE_COUNT + 1.
            EXIT. " Exit the inner loop once we've found and updated the matching record
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      " If no duplicate found, count as insert
      IF LV_KEY_MATCH = ABAP_FALSE.
        LV_INSERT_COUNT = LV_INSERT_COUNT + 1.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Remove the records marked for deletion (the duplicates we've already processed)
  LOOP AT <LT_ALV> ASSIGNING <LS_ALV>.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IS_DELETED' OF STRUCTURE <LS_ALV> TO <LV_FIELD>.
    IF SY-SUBRC = 0 AND <LV_FIELD> = 'X'.
      DELETE <LT_ALV> INDEX SY-TABIX.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  " 8. Display results
  IF LV_DUPLICATE_FOUND = ABAP_TRUE.
    LV_MSG = |Processed { LV_ROW - 1 } records: Updated { LV_UPDATE_COUNT } existing records, Added { LV_INSERT_COUNT } new records|.
    MESSAGE LV_MSG TYPE 'S'.
  ELSE.
    LV_MSG = |Successfully added { LV_INSERT_COUNT } new records|.
    MESSAGE LV_MSG TYPE 'S'.
  ENDIF.

  " 9. Refresh ALV display
  GV_DATA_CHANGED = ABAP_TRUE.
  IF GO_GRID IS BOUND.
    CALL METHOD GO_GRID->REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY
      EXPORTING
        IS_STABLE = VALUE #( ROW = ABAP_TRUE COL = ABAP_TRUE ).
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Logistics Private Cloud Edition FAQ - Solution deployment scenarios EWM-TM Integration
entry.content:
Efficient logistics processes are the backbone of globally operated supply chains. In the heart and centre of these solutions are for many of our customer warehouse and transportation management processes. An efficient integration of the involved solutions is the key to enable transparency, fast reaction on supply chain disruptions, changes business needs and adoption to environmental changes.

With SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud there are two options available to integrate solution components Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and Transportation Management (TM) available:

  • Transportation Unit-based integration
  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving (Adv. SR)

The following article considers primarily SAP S/4HANA integration. Please note that integration to SAP ECC versions is not part of this article.

While the article focuses on deployment scenarios, the decision when to choose which integration depends on multiple factors:

  • Technical Architecture: Performance and scalability, Data Center Strategy, deployment and instance strategy
  • Transformation strategy: Logistics transformation before, during or after SAP S/4HANA transformation, roll-out planning
  • Requirements on maintenance and operations: Independent innovation and upgrade cycles for warehouse and transportation management functions
  • Functional solution capabilities and integration design: Centralized or distributed transportation management capabilities (e.g. procurement, planning, settlement), communication within business network, transportation planning vs. load consolidation, integration of multiple different warehouse management solutions, etc.).

Options and Differences

To integration EWM with TM in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, there are two options:

  • Transportation Unit-based Integration

  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving

The decision which integration is the right one for your business can be evaluated along-side the aspects of integration objects and capabilities, interfaces, deployment options and supported scenarios.

To better understand the functional differences between both integration scenarios, have a look at SAP Note 3423370 - Feature Comparison of Adv. SR TM-EWM versus TU-Based TM-EWM Integration.

There are functional restrictions in the Integration Based on EWM Transportation Units to be further considered – see also SAP Note 3018355 - Supported functional scope and restrictions of the TU-based TM EWM Integration for shippers.

Typical Solution Architecture Variants

In the following section, we would like to discuss typical solution architecture variants and relevant integration-scenarios.

System consolidation

The system consolidation scenario can be characterised by running all enterprise function on one central SAP S/4HANA instance. Motivation for this scenario are typically consolidation and efficiency benefits in operations and maintenance.

Depending on functional scope relevant (see also SAP Note 3423370 - Feature Comparison of Adv. SR TM-EWM versus TU-Based TM-EWM Integration) the following scenarios are available.

  • Transportation Unit-based Integration

  • Advnaced Shipping and Receiving

Central Transportation Management System

Integrating multiple business units, which are running their own warehouse management systems and have to integrate to a central Transportation Management for transportation consolidation and planning.

  • Transportation Unit-based Integration

  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving

Side-car Logistics system

Logistics processes including warehouse and transportation management should be run on a side-car logistics system and integration to central SAP S/4HANA system.

  • Transportation Unit-based Integration - consider SAP Note 2812981 - Transportation Management and decentralized EWM in same SAP S/4HANA system and client for prerequisites and restrictions

  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving - available since SAP S/4HANA 2023, FPS00 with restrictions. See also SAP Note 3369482 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 Supply Chain - Advanced Shipping and Receiving for Decentralized EWM and TM - Release Information

Note: Decentral Advanced Shipping and Receiving Scenario is supported for Outbound Process exclusively since SAP S/4HANA release 2023 FPS01. Enhancement to Inbound Process is subject to roadmap planning.

Side-car Warehouse Management System

Due to high complexity, infrastructure integration and/or requirements against operations and maintenance cycles, a side-car warehouse management has to integrate with an embedded Transportation Management. From a Transportation Management perspective a centrally run transportation management capabilities (e.g. central procurement, planning and settlement) could as well be a reason for this deployment scenario.

Depending on the requirement to either run a central Transportation Management solution in SAP S/4HANA or to operate Transportation Management in side-car logistics system the following deployment options are possible:

  • Transportation Unit-based Integration

Summary

With this blog post we aim to give guidance to choose the right EWM - TM integration scenario depending on the specific requirements and considering the constrains give for your transformation project. The major aspects are the evaluation of deployment scenario, business capabilities and solution capabilities. As analysis of these aspects can lead to conflicting results it is recommended to discuss these with various stakeholders within your organization and discuss on a weighting of the relevant discussion criteria to find the solution best fitting to your business transformation strategy.

Further great resources on the topic:

SAP Community Blogs:

  • Adv. SR. - The New Simplified Integration Between SAP TM & SAP EWM
  • Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA, Private Cloud Edition – Essential Knowledge Base, Learnings and Tools

SAP Notes:

  • 3225241 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Setup Guide
  • 3512117 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Cookbook
  • 3271412 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving with Global Trade Service Integration - Setup Guide
  • 3081804 - Integrating Delivery Management with Advanced Shipping and Receiving in S/4HANA (Restrictions and Release Information)
  • 3369482 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 Supply Chain - Advanced Shipping and Receiving for Decentralized EWM and TM - Release Information
  • SAP Help: Overview of Features in Advanced Shipping and Receiving

SAP Learning:

  • Transformation from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC automation public cloud Netherlands
entry.content:
Hello @Danny5 

Good question, the setup for SAP DRC (Document and Reporting Compliance) automation for the Netherlands in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition indeed requires a valid digital certificate to enable automated VAT and EC Listing submissions to the Dutch tax authorities (Belastingdienst).

The type of certificate required depends on the technical channel and legal requirement:

For the Netherlands DRC automation, you typically need a Qualified Website Authentication Certificate (QWAC) or a Qualified Electronic Seal Certificate (QCert for ESeal). Here’s the quick distinction:

QWAC ,Used to establish secure communication channels (https/TLS) between your SAP Cloud system and the Dutch tax portal.

 QCert for ESeal ,Provides a legally recognized electronic seal (similar to a company stamp) to sign electronic documents, such as VAT returns or ECL declarations.

From experience and based on SAP documentation for DRC in NL:

  • The QWAC is often mandatory to authenticate your system with the tax authorities for secure API communication.
  • In some processes (Example, when digitally signing submitted documents), the QCert for ESeal may also be required.

It is recommended to confirm with your Trust Service Provider which certificates are included in their package providers usually offer both QWAC and ESeal options.

Check SAP S/4HANA Public cloud help Portal under

  • Document and Reporting Compliance → Netherlands → Electronic VAT Returns/ECL Submissions
  • SAP Help Portal - DRC (search for Netherlands-specific DRC setup)

Let me know if you need further support

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA - How to develop an SAPUI5 app calling a back...
entry.content: If it’s an integration scenario an app seems unnecessary but you are sending data from S/4HANA, it seems a very good use case for an event-driven architecture. If you have a piece of code in Abap that calls an external API, this is most likely not following clean core or a modern approach. Still, you would need to expose this as an OData service to consume in this app to reprocess stuff. If you are in S/4HANA public cloud or using a recent version in private cloud or on premise you can benefit from events in RAP and services such as event mesh in BTP. Still, you could create an app to call the same event of triggers the integration once again. https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/what-is-event-driven-architecture.html

entry.title: Securing Data Journey with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
(Jana Subramanian serves as  APJ Principal Cybersecurity and Compliance Advisor for Strategic Customer Engagements and has been recognized as a Fellow of Information Privacy (FIP) by the International Association of Privacy Professionals (IAPP). As part of his responsibilities, Jana helps with strategic customer engagements related to topics such as cybersecurity and compliance, data privacy, multi-cloud security integration architecture, contractual assurance, audit, and regulatory compliance)

Introduction:

Today, businesses need to make quick, smart decisions and automate tasks. To do this well, they need all their data organized and in one place. This is where SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) steps in. SAP Business Data Cloud is a fully managed Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution that enables organizations to consolidate, harmonize, and govern data from SAP applications and connected non-SAP sources in a unified, trusted landscape. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), powered by SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), is designed to help organizations make the most of their data—regardless of whether it originates from SAP systems or external sources. As a fully managed platform, it brings together core data services like SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to deliver real-time insights and unified data governance.

In this blog, we will explore how secure data flows are maintained within BDC and with integrated platforms like SAP Databricks. We will unpack how SAP embeds strong security controls across the architecture, including identity and access management, encryption, and federated governance. Key topics include the shared responsibility model, authentication and authorization mechanisms, zero-copy data sharing, and key management practices using SAP-managed or customer-owned encryption keys.

Shared Security Responsibility Model

In cloud computing, security is a shared responsibility. The shared responsibility model for SAP Business Data Cloud clearly defines which security domains are managed by SAP as the Cloud Service Provider (CSP) and which are under the customer’s control. This clear division is essential to prevent security gaps and ensure a strong, end-to-end security posture.

Customers leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) retain key responsibilities that directly influence the security of their data and applications. These responsibilities are primarily concentrated in the application, data, and access management layers, which sit above the core infrastructure. SAP, as the Cloud Service Provider (CSP), assumes comprehensive responsibility for the foundational security of the BDC platform — including the underlying infrastructure, platform services, and system-level controls. By abstracting these lower-level complexities, SAP enables customers to focus on securely configuring and governing their data and workloads within the platform.

 Figure 1: Shared Security Model – SAP Business Data Cloud

S.NoSecurity Domain / Responsibility AreaCustomer ResponsibilitiesSAP (Cloud Service Provider) Responsibilities
1Custom Application SecuritySecure development, input validation, runtime logic of business-specific applications built on SAP BTP. Application-level logging for data anomalies.Provides secure development tools, services, and APIs. Ensures secure platform runtime environment and API management.
2Connectivity & IntegrationCustomers are responsible for setting up secure connections to SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) using tools like the DP Agent, Smart Data Access (SDA), secure connectivity, or SAP Cloud Connector. They decide what data to share, manage user roles and permissions, and govern data products within their workspaces. Customers also need to monitor access using audit tools and take action when needed. While SAP secures the platform itself, customers control how their data is accessed and shared.SAP provides secure mechanisms such as Delta Sharing, Connection Management, secure connectors and agents, and IP allowlisting to enable trusted integration and data exchange.
3Authorization & Role ManagementDesign and maintain RBAC, segregation of duties. Configure user roles, spaces, permissions. Integrate corporate IDP. Manage user assignments, access policies at tenant level.Provide XSUAA/IAS for user authentication, SSO, OAuth2 tokens, and trust tools. Provide default templates/scopes (XSUAA, role collections, APIs).
4Identity & AuthenticationIntegrate enterprise IdP, enforce SSO/MFA, manage user lifecycle with SAP IAS. Align user lifecycle management with HR/IT policies for onboarding/off-boarding.Provide XSUAA/IAS for user authentication, SSO, OAuth2 tokens. Manage authentication through SAP Cloud Identity Services, Identity Provisioning (SCIM sync).
5Infrastructure SecurityNot applicable - focus on custom application layer.Responsible for managing the underlying hyperscaler infrastructure, which includes compute, storage, virtual machines (VMs), containers, networking, and cloud account security. SAP ensures high availability and redundancy, and it manages the infrastructure-as-a-service (IaaS) provider (such as AWS, Azure, or GCP) as a Sub-Processor under SAP’s data protection and compliance obligations.
6Platform Hardening and PatchingResponsible for ensuring apps/models built by customer are maintained and compatible with platform updates.Apply OS, middleware, database patches. Manage SAP BTP runtime environments and HANA Cloud infrastructure.
7Security Monitoring & Incident ManagementReport customer-side incidents via SAP for Me. Respond to alerts affecting business processes or data.Monitor user activities, access patterns, data flows. Handle platform-related incidents, provide communication and RCA.
8Audit and ComplianceMust ensure internal use of SAP BDC complies with industry/local regulations (e.g., MAS TRM, HIPAA, GDPR obligations).Ensure global compliance (ISO 27001, SOC 1/2, GDPR). Provide platform-level logging. Publish audit reports.
9Application Services & Database/Object Store SecurityNot applicable - focus on custom application layer.Secure foundational infrastructure and platform services for Datasphere, SAC, BW/4HANA, HANA Cloud, Object Store. Ensure encrypted data storage (backup/restore), access control.
10Platform and OrchestrationNot applicable - abstracted from customer.Manage Kubernetes orchestration, containersauto-scaling, patching across platform.
11Data EncryptionResponsible for secure connection to external data sources. Decide if BYOK/KMS setup is required.Ensure encryption at rest using SAP-managed KMS. TLS 1.2+ for all data in motion.
12Data IsolationMust structure data access and modeling to reflect internal segregation (e.g., business units, domains).Enforce tenant isolation at infrastructure, database (HANA), object store (HDFLS), and API layers.
13Data RecoveryResponsible for validating if data/model refresh is needed post-restore; no direct backup restore access.Perform automated backups, restoration, define RTO/RPO for applicable cloud services,  manage DR processes for platform-level resilience.
14Logging and MonitoringCan enable application-level logging responsible for alerting on data-level anomalies in models/reports.Monitor platform operations, security events, audit trails, system health.
15Data Governance and ModellingOwns the design, ownership, approval, and lifecycle of data models and product definitions. Must structure data access and modeling to reflect internal segregation.Provides tools (SAP Datasphere) to manage lineage, metadata, business semantics, data products.
16Application Security (Platform-level)Responsible for securing any custom apps, views, or integrations built on BDC.Secures built-in services, APIs, SDKs, and default UI components provided in BDC.
17User Role and Management (Platform-level)Manages user assignments, role definitions, space allocations, and access policies at tenant level.Provides default templates or scopes (XSUAA, role collections, scopes, APIs).
18Data Lifecycle and RetentionData lifecycle with internal data governance, privacy regulations, and industry policies.Offers retention tooling and data storage options; underlying infra follows SAP retention & purge policies.
19Vulnerability Management & Custom ExtensionsResponsible for securing extensions, handling alerts, and responding to advisories relevant to custom assets.SAP scans and remediates platform-level vulnerabilities; issues security advisories and patch notes. Secure Software Development lifecycle approach to application development (SAST/DAST/Secure Source Code Reviews)
20Third-Party SubprocessorsMust subscribe and review subprocessors during onboarding and monitor regulatory implications.Discloses subprocessors (e.g., hyperscalers) in the SAP My Trust Center; contracts and audits them.
21Service Availability and SLAMonitors usage against SLAs; plans business continuity around published SAP SLAs.Availability SLAs apply only to core capacity services, as defined in the SAP BDC Product Supplement.
22Change ManagementOwns change management for models, roles, and data sources inside their tenant.Controls release cycles for SAP-managed services (e.g., Datasphere, HANA Cloud).
23Data Residency/SovereigntyChooses the appropriate region for compliance; verifies data residency needs are fulfilled.Offers deployment in specific regions; enforces logical isolation.
24On-Boarding and Off-Boarding UsersMust align user lifecycle management with HR or IT policies (e.g., disable access when users leave).Provides platform hooks (e.g., IAS/IDP sync) and APIs for user lifecycle integration.
25Breach NotificationCustomer must respond, coordinate internal teams, assess business impact.Notifies customer of confirmed customer data security breaches incident under SAP DPA.
26Service DecommissioningClean up unused spaces, data products, and user roles.Decommissions infrastructure/instances upon tenant deletion.

Secure Architecture – Component View

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), delivers a unified and governed environment for enterprise data management and analytics. By integrating core services like SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, and SAP Analytics Cloud, it enables seamless data flow and harmonization across SAP and non-SAP sources. This architecture supports a wide spectrum of data needs—from traditional warehousing and reporting to AI-driven applications—while enforcing federated governance, identity, and secure access. BDC serves as a foundation for operational intelligence, allowing enterprises to build and scale intelligent data products and insights across their ecosystems.

Figure 2:  SAP BDC Component View

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) brings together various components and services to enable secure, scalable, and intelligent data operations across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. Data flows through BDC in well-defined paths—from ingestion, transformation, storage, and modeling, to analytics and advanced processing.

At the heart of BDC lies the Foundation Services (FOS), which connect to both SAP and non-SAP systems using secure, standards-based protocols. Data from SAP S/4HANA, SAP SaaS and other cloud/on-premise systems is securely ingested via mechanisms like the SAP BTP Cloud Connector, DP Agent, SDAs and HTTPS APIs. Once inside BDC, data is transformed, curated, and modeled using SAP Datasphere, and may optionally be exposed as trusted data products.

The platform supports zero-copy integration with SAP Databricks using Delta Sharing, ensuring secure AI/ML access without data duplication. SAP BW systems can also be modernized and integrated via direct provisioning into Datasphere. The BDC Cockpit serves as the central hub for managing roles, access control, and data governance.

To ensure end-to-end protection, all data flows are governed by robust security measures—including OAuth 2.0, SAML, TLS 1.2+, object-level access controls, encryption at rest and in transit, and logging/auditing mechanisms.

 

S.NoComponentPrimary FunctionContribution to BDC Ecosystem
1SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)Dashboards, planning, visualizations.Primary interface for analytics and intelligent applications, consuming data from Datasphere and BW.
2SAP DatasphereSemantic modeling, data federation, data lineage, data product generation.Central hub for trusted data, enabling data fabric capabilities and accelerating data product creation.
3SAP HANA Cloud (Compute and In-Memory)High-performance compute and caching layer.Essential for processing and analyzing large datasets, supporting Datasphere and Object Store.
4Object Store (SAP HANA Cloud, Data lake files)Primary data lake storage for unstructured/semi-structured data.Underpins data lake capabilities, providing scalable and flexible data storage.
5SAP DatabricksAdvanced ML/AI capabilities, data engineering.Enables zero-copy integration via Delta Sharing; applies ML/AI directly on curated SAP data products
6SAP BW Private Cloud Edition (BW/4HANA or BW 7.5)Modernization of existing SAP BW data to the cloud.Ensures continuity for established BW landscapes, serving as a private cloud data provisioning cockpit.
7SAP BDC CockpitCentral administration, configuration, security, cataloging, identity management.Orchestrates and manages the entire BDC environment, providing a single control plane. Unified control plane for managing tenants, users, trust, and metadata across BDC
8Foundational Services (FOS)Data acquisition, transformation, curation, storage, IAM, monitoring, logging, KMS.Provides core shared capabilities, underpinning secure and efficient operation of all higher-level BDC components.

  Figure 3: Authentication and Authorization

Security in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is built into every step of the data journey—from ingestion to storage, processing, and final use by analytics and intelligent applications. SAP uses a multi-layered security approach to ensure that data is protected at all times—whether it’s moving between systems or stored within the platform.

Data is secured through encrypted channels, strong access controls, and logging. BDC protects information both in transit and at rest, with role-based access and tenant isolation to help meet enterprise security and compliance requirements.

 

Figure 4: Secure Data Flow

The table below summarizes how data flows across BDC, the purpose of each flow, and the security mechanisms in place at every step.

S NoFlowPurposeProtocols / Security ControlsDescription
1SAP Source Systems → Foundational Services (FOS)Ingest data from SAP systems like S/4HANA or ECCSAP Cloud Connector (outbound-only, TLS 1.2+), DP Agent + SDI for structured replicationData from SAP source systems (e.g., S/4HANA or ECC) is securely ingested into SAP Business Data Cloud’s Foundational Services layer using the SAP Cloud Connector, which enforces outbound-only, TLS 1.2+ encrypted communication. For structured replication, Data Provisioning (DP) Agent and Smart Data Integration (SDI) are utilized.
2Non-SAP Systems → Foundational Services (FOS)Ingest data from APIs, flat files, or third-party systemsHTTPS (TLS 1.2+), OAuth2-based API access, File upload with access control and metadata taggingData from external sources—such as APIs, flat files, or third-party systems—is ingested into the Foundational Services layer. Security controls include HTTPS (TLS 1.2+) for secure transmission, OAuth2-based API access for authentication, and file uploads with integrated access control and metadata tagging
3Foundational Services → SAP DatasphereDeliver curated and transformed data for modelingInternal secure flow, Object-level access control, Tenant isolation, AES-256 encryptionOnce data is within the Foundational Services layer, it is curated and transformed for downstream consumption. The flow to SAP Datasphere is protected by internal security mechanisms, including object-level access control, robust tenant isolation, and AES-256 encryption to safeguard data at rest and in transit
4Foundational Services → SAP BW/4HANA or BW 7.5 PCEPush Info Providers for provisioning as data productsODP/ABAP Adapter, HTTPS/TLS, SAP role-based security on BW objectsInfo Providers are pushed from FOS to SAP BW/4HANA or BW 7.5 Private Cloud Edition for provisioning as data products. Security measures include the ODP/ABAP Adapter, HTTPS/TLS for secure communication, and SAP role-based security applied directly on BW objects [Image 8]. The Foundational Services layer acts as a secure ingress and egress point for data within BDC, functioning as a critical security enforcement gateway. It enforces essential security policies and controls before data is distributed to other components like Datasphere or BW. This centralized enforcement point simplifies overall security management and ensures consistent application of policies across diverse data sources, significantly reducing the likelihood of misconfigurations or unauthorized data flows.
5SAP BW/4HANA PCE → SAP DatasphereOnboard legacy BW models/data into DatasphereData Product Generator, Delta Tables over object store, Logged provisioningThe onboarding of legacy BW models and data into SAP Datasphere occurs via this flow. This process leverages the Data Product Generator and Delta Tables over the object store, with detailed logging for provisioning activities to ensure auditability [Image 8].
6SAP Datasphere → SAP DatabricksShare curated data for ML/AI and engineeringDelta Sharing over HTTPS, Token-based authentication, Secure zero-copy accessCurated data for Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) engineering is shared. This is performed via Delta Sharing over HTTPS, utilizing token-based authentication for secure access and enabling secure zero-copy access to minimize data duplication risks [Image 8]. This indicates a modern and highly efficient approach to data sharing that prioritizes both security and performance. "Zero-copy access" is particularly significant as it avoids data duplication, which not only reduces storage costs but also significantly minimizes the risk surface associated with multiple, potentially unsynchronized copies of sensitive data.
7SAP Databricks → Datasphere (optional feedback loop)Return scored or enriched datasetsSecure API-based integration, Enforced token policies and loggingWhen scored or enriched datasets are returned from SAP Databricks to Datasphere, this feedback loop enables continuous learning and re-integration into data products or analytics pipelines. Secure APIs ensure that data flows back into Datasphere with enforced token policies, role checks, and audit logging to maintain trust and governance throughout the cycle.
8SAP BDC Cockpit → All ComponentsAdminister roles, catalog, governanceOAuth2 / SAML2.0 authentication, SAP IAS integration, Audit loggingThe central administration point manages roles, the data catalog, and governance across all BDC components. Security is enforced through OAuth2/SAML2.0 authentication, integration with SAP IAS/IAG (Identity and Access Governance), and extensive audit logging for all administrative action.
9SAP Analytics Cloud → SAP Datasphere and BW PCEConsume dashboards, stories, planning modelsHTTPS + OAuth2/SAML, Inherits row-level and object-level data permissionsDashboards, stories, and planning models are consumed from SAP Datasphere and BW Private Cloud Edition. This consumption is secured via HTTPS + OAuth2/SAML, and permissions inherit existing row-level and object-level data permissions defined in Datasphere and BW, ensuring fine-grained access control.
10Foundational Services → Data StorePersist data with security and compliance guaranteesAES-256 encryption at rest, Encryption in transit (TLS), KMS (SAP-managed, CMEK planned)Data persisted with robust security and compliance guarantees. This includes AES-256 encryption at rest, encryption in transit (TLS), and Key Management Services (KMS), with SAP-managed keys and Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) planned for enhanced control 
11SAP BDC Core Platform Layer (Internal)Orchestrate cross-component interactionsSecure APIs, Tenant-aware routing, RBAC/ABAC enforcement across servicesThe internal orchestration of cross-component interactions within the BDC core platform layer is secured using secure APIs, tenant-aware routing to ensure data isolation between customers, and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) / Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) enforcement across all services 

Conclusion:

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) provides a secure and unified foundation for enterprise data and AI initiatives, integrating key components such as SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Databricks, and SAP BW/4HANA. Built on a shared responsibility model, SAP ensures infrastructure security, while customers focus on governing data and enforcing access policies. This represents a strategic shift from managing infrastructure to securing data products—structured, governed assets that accelerate analytics and AI use cases. The platform emphasizes resiliency through multi-Availability Zone (AZ) deployments, robust backup and restore capabilities, and defined RTO/RPO for BW/4HANA PCE to support business continuity.

Security is embedded throughout the architecture with end-to-end encryption (at rest and in transit), granular access controls, and continuous monitoring. The Foundational Services layer acts as a policy enforcement gateway, enabling secure and efficient data movement and transformation across components. Combined with modern data-sharing capabilities like secure zero-copy access, BDC allows organizations to innovate confidently, make informed decisions, and unlock the full value of their data with trust and governance at the core.

entry.title: Re: Unable to use reference field in bank statement memo line
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question, which I have forwarded to the experts.

Until they answer the question, the following information may be useful:

Alternative Field for Reference in Bank Statement

When dealing with the Reprocess Bank Statements app, if the reference field in the sales order memo line is insufficient due to its limitation of 16 characters, you might consider using other fields that can accommodate more characters.

Possible Solutions:

  1. Custom Fields and Logic App: You can extend the app using the Custom Fields and Logic app. Receivables Management - App Extensibility: Reprocess Bank Statement Items This allows you to add custom fields to the UI elements, which might provide the necessary space for your reference data.

  2. Key User Extensibility: Utilize the key user extensibility features to create custom fields that can be used in the memo line or other relevant sections of the bank statement processing.

  3. Alternative Fields: Check if there are other fields available in the bank statement processing that can be used for storing extended reference information, such as the ‘Additional Information’ field in QR-bill processing, QR-Bill in SAP S/4HANA Cloud which allows up to 140 characters.

Please also refer to the following information:

App Extensibility: Reprocess Bank Statement Items

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Payment Medium Format .txt creation (ASCII (comma sep...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I hope the following information is helpful:

To create a flat file format (.txt, ASCII comma-separated) using the Map Format Data app, you can follow these steps:

  1. Understand the Structure: First, ensure you have a clear understanding of the structure provided by the bank. This includes the order of fields, data types, and any specific formatting requirements.

  2. Use Map Format Data App:

    • Open the Map Format Data app in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment.
    • Use the ‘Create Payment Medium Formats’ feature to start a new format.

  3. Define Nodes and Attributes:

    • Define the nodes required for your format. This includes record groups, records, and fields.
    • Set attributes for each node, such as data type, length, and mandatory status.

  4. Configure Output Specifications:

    • Specify how each field should be outputted in the flat file. This includes setting delimiters (comma for CSV) and ensuring the correct sequence.

  5. Test the Format:

    • Use the ‘Test Incoming Format Mappings’ tool to simulate the format and ensure it meets the bank’s requirements.
    • Adjust any discrepancies found during testing.

  6. Release and Use:

    • Once satisfied with the format, release it for use.
    • Assign the format to the relevant payment method.

You can refer to general resources on creating custom format mappings:

  • SAP Help Portal: Map Format Data for Payments

  • SAP Community Blog on Map Payment Format Data Map Payment Format Data - Video guide - SAP Community provides a video guide on how to use the Map Payment Format Data app.

  • SAP Note 3309184 offers test data and examples for learning to use Map Format Data.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: 记账码字段状态可以更改吗，如何更改
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question! Can you please check whether the following information is helpful?

The posting key field status can be changed, but it is subject to certain system configurations and restrictions. The field status is controlled by the account group and field status variant settings in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

How to Change Posting Key Field Status:

  • Access Field Status Variant: Navigate to the configuration step for defining field status variants (SSCUI 102393). Here, you can view and modify the field status settings.
  • Create Custom Field Status Group: How to Use the ‘Post Automatically Only’ Indicator… - SAP Community If changes are not allowed in the standard field status groups, you can create a custom field status group by copying an existing one and making necessary adjustments.
  • Assign Custom Group: Assign the newly created custom field status group to the relevant G/L account in the Manage G/L Account Master Data app (SSCUI 150001).

    Entering Activity Type Field in Accounting Document

     If the activity type field is allowed in the field status group but not visible on the input interface, it might be due to the configuration of the input screen or missing field assignments.

    Steps to Resolve:

    • Check Field Status Group: SAP S/4HANA Cloud : How to add additional account … - SAP Community Ensure that the field status group assigned to the G/L account includes the activity type field as optional or required.
    • Screen Configuration: Verify the screen layout configuration for the accounting document entry to ensure the activity type field is included.
    • Custom Fields and Logic: Use the Custom Fields and Logic app to add the activity type field to the input interface if it is missing.

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: End of Support Date for SAP Crystal Reports for Visua...
entry.content:
You can get all sorts of info including end of life from this page:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/crystal-reports/faq

End of mainstream Maintenance and Priority one Support dates can be found here: https://wiki.scn.sap.com/wiki/display/BOBJ/SAP+Crystal+Products+-+End+of+Mainstream+Maintenance+Dates

Note the EOL for Crystal Reports progresses, so the EOL date for current builds gets extended as the years pass...

Yes SP 36 and 37 has issues, download SP 38 to see if it resolves any issues you may be having:

Go here to read the latest for CR for VS:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

Typically SAP supports the current less 2 releases/patches.

CR for VS does NOT get patched so always use the latest SP, if you see the issue after updating to the latest release report it here and I'll get R&D to look into the bug you found.

FYI - the Product Own and SDK owner does monitor this site so if I'm not responding he may....

entry.title: Code Connect 2025: UI5con, reCAP, HANA Tech Con
entry.content:
This blog post summarizes the most important information about Code Connect 2025. It will be updated with further information (e.g. Event App, links to recordings...).

Code Connect 2025 – three days, three conferences, one streamlined journey

Join us for the second annual  Code Connect, where we're bringing together the hottest tech events in one convenient location. Be ready to connect, collaborate, and code away with a bustling community of like-minded developers. Jump on the Code Connect train and elevate your conference experience!

  • Find more information here: https://code-connect.dev/ 
  • Event App: will be added soon

Code Jams & Warmup

UI5con

reCAP

HANA Tech Con

7th of July8th of July9th of July10th of July
code-connect.devopenui5.org/ui5conrecap-conf.devhanatech.community
See details in text belowAgendaAgendaAgenda
Live Streams: n/aLive Stream (Main Stage)

Live Stream (Main Stage)

Live Stream (Side Track)

Live Stream (Main Stage)

Live Stream (Side Track)

Recordings: n/aRecordings (will be added)Recordings (will be added) Recordings (will be added)

Code Jams & Warmup (July 7th)

Are you passionate about front-end development and have experience with OpenUI5 applications? Then don’t miss out on our OpenUI5-focused CodeJam! Join Peter Muessig and Nico Schönteich as they dive into advanced OpenUI5 topics. You’ll experience TypeScript, setting up a mono repository, OData V4 caveats, integrating 3rd-party packages, creating custom controls and libraries, and testing strategies.

Prefer back-end development? Our second CodeJam is tailored just for you! Led by DJ Adams, Daniel Schlachter, and Johannes Vogt, this session will explore the powerful SAP Application Programming Model (CAP). You’ll learn to integrate external services, work with CDS for declaring and relating entities, and leverage the SAP Cloud SDK for connectivity and destinations. In addition, we’ll also dive into new CAP features. Check the essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location.

Join the Official Unofficial Pre-Event for Code Connect 2025!

  • Right after the UI5- and CAP-CodeJams - starting from 05:00 PM on Monday, July 7
  • Location: Restaurant ACE at Golf Club at St. Leon Rot, just a short walk from ROT03
  • See more details here

  (July 8th)

Your journey to UI5 mastery begins at UI5con, the ultimate destination for developers of all levels.

Get ready to navigate through a network of knowledge, discover the latest UI5 innovations, and make new friendships along the way. With a map full of expert sessions and interactive workshops, you'll connect with the UI5 community and its creators, exchanging valuable insights and experiences. Are ready to dive into the UI5 universe? UI5con is your ticket to ride!

Learn more about UI5con

 (July 9th)

Join the awesome developer conference all about the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). The time of the year where our lively communities, customers, and partners meet the CAP Product Team and exchange best practices, technical concepts, current projects, ideas for the future, and way more.

Learn more about reCAP

 (July 10th)

Get Connected! If there is anything about SAP HANA you always wanted to know but never dared to ask - this is the place for you! Be part of the community of development experts, users and partners to exchange, ignite and delight.

Get Involved! If you want to speak up in your own session worthwhile to be shared with this great community. Looking forward to see you there soon!

Learn more about HANA Tech Con

entry.title: Re: Report shipping error
entry.content:
Hello

(highlevel overview only)

first: a WWI report is generated via SAP transaction CG02

second: a WWI report is released SAP transactin CG50

third: you are referring to SAP transaction CVD1. Here only the "final" report is generated and not the raw report

Forth: potentially: you have to ensure the DMS and WWI servers are set up correct and any job is running successfully

I am sorry. There are to many reasons why the issue which you have determined is coming up in CVD1. Even if you would show the full customizing: on top we would need "job net information" and many other stuff.

So I am sorry. No further help possible

C.B.

entry.title: One-Click Export-Automating SuccessFactors EC Standard New Hire Integration API Document
entry.content:
Hi Successfolks!

In this blog, I will introduce how to utilize an standard and reusable Python script to form the SuccessFactors Employee Central New Hire Integration API document based on SF metadata and API response extraction. This aims to streamline EC API document output and maximum the efficiency.

Why Auto-form SF EC API Integration Document Matter?

Background:

As an Integration Consultant, I regularly develop customized API documentation for customer-specific SuccessFactors integration scenarios, which I provide to downstream systems as technical reference. One of the most frequent and complex integration requirements involves new hire data synchronization - particularly when customers employ third-party recruiting solutions that must integrate with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding (ONB) or Employee Central (EC). While the core HRIS element structure remains largely consistent across implementations, I currently spend significant time manually generating these new hire integration documents for each customer. Recognizing this as a repeatable process with predictable patterns, I plan to implement an automated solution to generate draft documentation. The automation will leverage standardized templates while accommodating necessary variations in customer-specific requirements, ultimately improving both efficiency and documentation quality.

Introduction:

With the power of SF OData API and Python, it is achievable to utilize through the following key steps:

  1. Extract SF EC master data metadata as mentioned in the blog: One-Click Export-Automating SuccessFactors EC API Metadata to Excel 
  2. Extract SF EC master data API Response.
  3. Re-organize the API response and API data dictionary.
  4. Mass upsert the re-structured API body based on specific rule and Capture Response.
  5. Fine Tune for the draft API document.

Python Script

Document Template

Benefit

Auto-output the standard new hire integration API into a clean and formatted Excel document via a python script:
Key benefits of automation:

  • Automated and Streamlined Process
    Significantly reduces manual effort in exporting the OData API dictionary.
  • Lightweight and Reusable
    Can be reused across instances by adjusting variables in the script if no customization is needed.
  • Real-time responsiveness
    Enables mass updates to API document instantly with one click once there exist new entity or field integration.

Key Concepts Behind the Solution

  1. Almost same standard EC HRIS Master Data Structure are enabled for different customer
  2. Body in Post API almost is the same with the API response in Get API after conversion
  3. upsertable attribute in Entity identify all fields which could appear in Body of post API.

How to Get Started

Prerequisite

  1. Python environment (Refer to online tutorials for setup on Windows or Mac)
  2. SF instance API endpoint and credentials.
  3. A sample use case with full employee profile data.
    Please get necessary script via github: https://github.com/Berg-Song/SuccessFactors_API_Metadata_Extraction/tree/main

Extract SF EC Master Data Metadata

Run the first and second script to get the metadata for EC entity and fields and remove the unimportant column.
Please follow the blog to complete the task: One-Click Export-Automating SuccessFactors EC API Metadata to Excel

Extract SF EC Master Data API Response

Run the third script to get the API Response of all entities for one employee(https://{API_SERVER}/odata/v2/{entity}?&format=json&̲filter={filter_field} eq '{EmployeeId}) and remove the unnecessary key for upsert. Below show some important logic:

Different API query endpoint and key for different entity.

  1. userId for entity is started with "Emp" and "Background".
  2. personExternalId for entity is started with Per
  3. usersSysId for entity is equal to EmpCostDistribution
  4. worker for entity is equal to PaymentInformationV3

Remove the key-value in Body which is not upsertable.

  1. The key is started with created or lastModified, or contain "Nav" keyword.(Exclude Navigation field)
  2. The content in _metadata.url().

Extract SF EC Master Data API Response

Re-organize API Response and API data Dictionary

  1. Combine the sample value and field attribute.
  2. Remove the non-upsertable field from sample upsert body and data dictionary.
  3. Replace the value in boday with testing data, such as employee Id, position, hire date and so on as needed.

Re-Structure API Response and API data Dictionary

Mass Upsert the Re-structured API body And Capture Response

  1. Post API with valid upsertable body and capture response.
  2. Form the overall entity table and upsert API field attribute.
  3. Auto-populated the sample post API using the built-in API template.

Documentation-Overview TableDocumentation-API Upsert DictionaryDocumentation-API Template

Final Thoughts:

Now you have get the standard new hire integration document based on your instance with a fixed template. And even, you could make it as project integration delivery document.
Note: You could use AI copilot to fine tune the script based on the requirement. Take an example, add user-based MDF into the scope or remove more specific key.

Future Plan

Another important item is about the dropdown list (MDF or Picklist)value mapping table, which I don't develop it as of now. It is expected that I will enhance it later, then the dropdown list involved in OData API Dictionary could also be retrieved and updated real time.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 2.0 - Recruit to hire data mapping validat...
entry.content:
Hi @Rac ,

When the Recruit to Hire Data Mapping produces a validation error during Onboarding 2.0, but does not explicitly show which field is causing the issue, follow these structured steps:

1. Review Recruit to Hire Data Mapping Thoroughly
Go to Admin Center → Recruit to Hire Data Mapping.

Focus on the Job Information section mapping.

Look for:

Mandatory fields in Employee Central Job Info not mapped properly.

Field format mismatches (e.g., string in RCM mapped to picklist in EC).

Picklist values in RCM that may not exist or be active in EC.

Tip: Pay extra attention to fields like:

Company

Location

Job Classification

Pay Group

Employment Type

Event Reason

2. Check EC Job Information Configuration
Go to Manage Business Configuration (BCUI).

Review the Job Information portlet:

Identify mandatory fields.

Confirm default values, field formats, and picklist associations.

If a mandatory field in EC does not have valid data coming from RCM, this can trigger mapping errors.

3. Validate Picklist Consistency
Ensure that picklist values used in Recruiting (RCM) align with those configured in Employee Central (EC).

If using MDF Picklists, ensure:

Picklists are active.

Translations are maintained (sometimes missing translations cause errors).

Cross-check picklist values for fields like:

Employment Type

Pay Scale Area

Location

4. Run Minimal Test Case
Create a test candidate with only the minimum required data in Recruiting.

Observe if onboarding triggers successfully.

Gradually add more fields to isolate which specific field might be breaking the process.

5. Browser Developer Tool (Advanced)
Although technical, here's what to look for:

Open Chrome DevTools → Network Tab.

Perform the Initiate Onboarding action.

Look for failed requests with HTTP 400 or 500 errors.

Expand the payload or response body; search for terms like fieldMapping or ValidationError.

Sometimes, the error message mentions a field name in technical format, such as:

jobInfo.company

jobInfo.location

If you're unable to interpret this, you can share the screenshot with SAP Support.

6. Check Business Rules (if applicable)
Review if any Business Rules are triggered during onboarding that set or validate Job Information fields.

Incorrect logic in rules can cause failures, especially with null values or incorrect data types.

Hope this helps!!

If this doesn't resolve your issue kindly raise SAP incident.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.
